All righty, hey, I've watched Russell sell products he knows nothing about many times. Think about that. The product rarely sells itself. Okay? Don't fall in love with the product so much that you forget how to sell a thing. That's what this whole event's been about. So while, yes, the product has to be amazing and you make it into an offer and it does everything that you promise it will do and more, you've got to make sure that you understand that your product is not good enough to sell itself. The stack itself usually takes him about 30 to 40 minutes. He's going to dive deep with us for a couple of hours on this. Guys, please stand up. Welcome to stage, Mr. Russell Brunson. So I'm just going to recap what we've done in the last two days because it's been a lot, but it's also not that much. Okay? So I know you guys are going to slide by slide home and there's a lot of stuff and figuring things out. It takes a while, right? But we break it down to the essence. What we've done so far is you guys have figured out your origin story, your story about your vehicle, internal and your external, right? So again, the whole structure of the perfect webinar is that. It's telling four stories. If I've done it right, the dominoes knocked over, they believe that this is the right vehicle for them. They believe that they can do it and they believe that all external false beliefs are away. Now they're like, and you'll see people in the chat box saying, like, just take my money. Like it literally will happen almost every single time because they've got belief now. And now all you have to do is make them a really, really good offer. Don't screw it up. Okay? If you're not careful, you'll screw it up. People will get so annoyed that they'll just not buy from you. And I learned that the hard way by not making a lot of money for a long time. And then luckily I met people who understood this process, who were good at it, who coached me through it. And then for the last decade, since then, it's been always learning and adding and learning and adding and learning and adding to where it's at today. And that's what we're going to be sharing with you guys. One of the most important stories I want to tell before we dive into this whole thing is the concept you've heard me talk about before is called the stack. And the reason why we call it the stack is I learned it from a guy named Armand Moran and he called it the stack. So that's it. It was interesting because I had been speaking at events for probably a year and a half or so at that time and I got on stage and I was good. I wasn't great, but I was doing good. I was able to convince people to buy and it was a good living. And Armand Moran had at the time the biggest event in our industry. I always wanted to speak at it my whole life. It was the first thing I ever went to and I was like, ah, somebody I want to speak at this event. And like four or five years into it, he asked me if I'd speak and I was so excited. And so I was preparing for getting ready and two weeks before his event, he was speaking at a Dan Kennedy event. So once Dan Kennedy's event and they had like 1500 people in the room or something. And Armand spoke on day four after like 200 speakers had all pitched up to this audience and I watched person after person and like half of them didn't do didn't sell very well and just kind of ups and downs of the whole thing. And a couple did okay, but for most part it was kind of bad. And Armand got there the last day and I was like, these guys are all beat up. Like it was one of those kind of, it was been to those kind of events. Yeah, I met you at one of those events. Yes. And I came in saying that for day four of that event and I came in and closed everybody and then that's when we met you guys. Anyway, so Armand came in to sing and I was just like, good luck man. These guys have been beat up. And he's like, I'm not worried. And he gets there, he does a little presentation and he sells and at the end I see the entire audience like probably 40 to 50% of people run to the back of the room. They're buying stuff. I'm just watching like an amazement. I have no idea what he did or how he did. I was just like, that was the coolest thing I ever saw. And then I kind of hung out just waiting for him to talk to me afterwards for all the people to kind of leave. And then he saw me in the corner. So he started walking over to me and first thing he did is he grabbed his, his a stack of order forms. And when you speak on stage, they give you a big stack of order forms and every order form is equivalent of like a thousand dollar bill or $2,000, depending on what you're spelling. Right? He's got this big old fat stack of order forms. He comes over to me and I was like, that's your order forms? He's like, yeah man, I guess how much that is. I was like, I don't know. And it was like 300 and something thousand dollars. Something great. I was just like, dude, that is like, like, that, like I couldn't even like fathom it. And he's like, hey, in two weeks, you speak my event, do you want a big stack of order forms or do you want a small stack of order forms? I was like, I want a big stack of order forms. He's like, okay, this is the difference between a big stack of order forms and a small stack. The small stack, you'll do your clothes the way you do it right now. Big stack you do, if you want a big stack of order forms, you do the stack that I do it. I'm like, okay, what do you do? He's like, didn't you see me just do it? I'm like, I think so. I don't know what you did, but it worked really, really good. And he explained this concept to me. He said that almost every speaker at the time in the speaking industry was out there. What they would do is they go through whatever, and everyone's got different versions of this. This is kind of my blend of what I figured out. So they've, everyone's got something different here. They teach whatever happens in the front and they transition to selling. The typical is they, they'll have some kind of package they put together. So this is the first thing you're going to get is so awesome. This is what you want. And then here's the second thing you're going to get. And here is the third thing you're going to get and here's the fourth thing you're going to get and here's the fifth thing you're going to get and then here is my special bonus you're going to get. It's going to cost you this. I'm not trying to charge you that, I'm gonna charge you that and then they sell. The problem is our human mind can only remember the very last thing I was told. Okay, can't remember all these things. So what happens is that you talk about this thing, and this is cool thing, and this is cool thing, and this is cool thing, and this is cool thing and then the last thing you which is like bonus 22, which is like the stupidest thing in your entire offer, and then you're like, the last bonus, and then it costs $15,000, and people are like, that's not worth $15,000, and all the only thing they remember is the last thing you offered him, okay? So he said what he invented was this thing called the stack. He said the way the stack works is basically, I talk about the first thing in my offer, I get them all excited, tell a story, share testimonials, and then I have a slide called a stack slide, and I said basically this means you're gonna get this, and I have the first thing in there. Then I transition to the second thing, I talk about it, go through the whole thing, and then when I'm done I go back and say, so that means you're gonna get this and this, and I stack it, okay? Then here's the third thing you're gonna get, and that means you're gonna get this, this, and this, the fourth thing, and you go through it. So at the very end, before I transitioned the price, I said, I go to the very last thing, and that means you're gonna get boom, boom, boom, boom, you see this huge entire thing, and you're like holy cow, that's the whole thing, and that's what's anchoring to the price. And all of a sudden the entire offer is actually worth the thing, and that was the stack. He said do that, so I went home, and I didn't really remember how he did it, but I was trying to figure it out, so I did it, went to the next two weeks later, spoke on stage at $160,000 in sales in 90 minutes, and the way it works, you get half, and the speaker gets half, or the event promoter gets half, so I walked home with $80,000 in order forms, and I had never made $80,000 in an hour my whole life, let alone my entire year, and I was just like, oh my gosh, the stack is the greatest thing in the world. I will never not do this, and I didn't for a while, so I started going back and I started adding it to my tele seminars back then, and webinars, and started using it for a long time, but something weird happened, and this is gonna happen to you guys, so I'm warning you right now, because if you listen to this voice, then your head that'll come, I promise you it'll come, and you will lose all the money. If you ignore this voice, you'll continue to make money. I kept doing it, and then one day I was like, I feel so cheesy doing this, it's so goofy, like I don't like it, I'm just gonna not do it anymore. I'm too sophisticated for this, or whatever I thought, I don't know, so I stopped doing the stack, and I tried it once in an event, and I bombed, nobody, nobody moved. I tried it again, and I tried it again after three or four times, I was like, okay, I am now making a rule for myself, I will never sell anything ever again in the history of my life without doing the stack, and I haven't since, okay? It works, it works magic, and the first time you feel goofy, it'll be kind of weird, but the difference between like $10,000, and $100,000, or $100,000, and a million, it's worth it every single time, just do it, get good at it, practice it, practice it in the mirror, whatever it takes, it's the key, okay? It's what, when this domino comes down, it causes so much desire that they just have to give you money, okay? So that's the foundation where we talk about. So I'm transitioning, if you look at how people's brains work, right? In NLP it talks about, there's two types of people, right? There's people that move towards pleasure and there's people who move away from pain. How many of you guys make the decisions in your life moving towards pleasure? How many of you guys make decisions in your life moving away from pain? Okay, typically most people are hardwired for one or the other, okay? The reason why most entrepreneurs screw up their presentation, most entrepreneurs make their decisions because they want to move towards pleasure, most of society makes their decisions because they want to move away from pain. Okay, so we come in there like, you're gonna make a million dollars a day and you're gonna be rich, you're gonna, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, right? Or you come in there like, you're gonna have a sexy body and ripped abs and da, da, da, da, da, right? Very small percentage of the audience is like, sweet, I want to live on a boat and I want to have six pack abs and all those things, right? But because we're entrepreneurs and that's what drives us, that's why we always pitch hard, okay? So you have to understand that we are not the majority of most people. Some people are there, so if you look at this the way I did it is I painted a picture for people who are moving towards pleasure, like what an extra, you guys make $150,000 a day, what would that be like to make $150,000 a day? Am I like 10% of my moving towards pleasure? People are like, oh my gosh, I'm getting a plane, a jet, a boat, a yacht, and it's gonna be the most amazing in the world, right? And everyone else is like, I just hate my boss. So then I go to the next level, the next level, the next level, the bottom end of this, I'm just like, man, an extra like $100 a day, that's $3,000 a month, what'd you do with that? They pay off your credit card bills, that get you out of pain? Could you fire your boss? Could you actually make this your job? Work at home with your kids? And my away from pain people are like, oh my gosh, like if I could fire my boss, I hate that guy. That'd be awesome, right? So I'm talking to both ends of the spectrum here, okay? Be very, very careful if you're only talking towards pleasure or away from pain. The blend of the two is the key because just everyone acts differently. And most entrepreneurs lean towards the pleasure and that's why they alienate a lot of their audiences in every market, okay? All right, so that's the one. Any questions about that slide at all? Now they've got it, now I'm gonna start transitioning over to the close, okay? And this is where most people get super nervous and super awkward. I see stage presenters all the time, they get down with the thing, they're teaching, and all of a sudden they're like, I'm gonna sell something. And they get really, really nervous. They start freaking out, they start hyperventilating, they start, yeah, and then they apologize and they feel all nervous and it's just like the most awkward train wreck in the world. Even my favorite time ever, I was at some people that go to church with us, call us, and were like, hey, we're doing a doTERRA party at our house, you wanna come and we'll try on some essential oils. And I was like, my wife's like, I don't know if I'm gonna go, I hate those kind of parties. I was like, please let me go with you, please, I wanna go so bad. And so I was the only dude in the room full of women and they're all trying on oils. And I was just like, she was really good presenting. And then it got to the point where she was gonna start selling something. And all of a sudden she starts talking faster and getting nervous, her face turns all red. And she's like, and if you guys wanna buy a little, a little, a little bit, and says like, I don't know, like probably a 20 minute pitch in like two and a half seconds. And like, I'm so sorry, but, and like, if you want on the forums back there, but don't worry about it, you don't have to buy it, but if you want them there, and like, I gotta go. And like just ended. I was like, I felt so bad for her, because like, that's the hardest thing, we don't know how to like transition, okay? And so, and I struggled with it for a long time. So I have, I feel the pain of like, how do you make that awkward transition? How many of you guys have done a presentation and you've gotten stuck in that awkward spot? Okay, so that's how I do it, it's super easy. Okay, you get done teaching, it's holding, you recap it, and you stop, and all you say is, let me ask you a question real quick. That's the transition. You guys all say it? Let me ask you a question real quick. Okay, that's it. And they're like, yeah, what? And then I try to have something there to kind of break the thing. So for me, I usually show this picture right here. So I say, let me ask you a question real quick. We're gonna go for an hour. How are we gonna get to feel a little bit like this right now? I never laugh. I know I talk really, really fast and I get excited. I know I've crammed so much stuff in the last hour, but I wanted to give as much information as possible to you during the time we had together. One time I was speaking at an event and I got off stage and this little man ran up to me and said, Russell, this is so exciting. I feel like I had a fire hose in my mouth the entire time. And next week some guy on my team sent me this picture. I was like, that's what people feel like after they talk to me, right? How do you guys feel a little bit like that? Okay, I totally get it. So because of that, I don't want you to leave overwhelmed. I just want to inspire you and show you what's possible. So what I actually did is I, and then this comes back to if I'm on an event, I talked to Grant Cardone and we figured out a package we could put together so that you guys can take this stuff and when you get back home, you can actually implement it. Are you guys a cool five-spend, about 10 or 15 minutes, walking through the special offer made for you guys? Absolutely. Good. And I stop. Don't get nervous. I ask their permission. This is the key. This is the secret. If you don't ask for their permission, it's awkward people will start popping up and walking out and they will leave in the middle of your presentation. It's the worst thing in the world and you get more nervous. You see people leaving. It's just super weird and super awkward. So I acknowledged. I said, look, I know that you couldn't figure it. I showed you what's possible, but you need to figure out how to actually do this. And I can't do that in a 90-minute presentation I have here. We've got to do this offline. I want to go with you to do that. And so because of that me and then whoever the promoter was, or if it's my own, we put together stuff. If it's my own, I say, hey, we put together a really special offer for you. Or if I'm a host with somebody else, I was talking so-and-so and we had a special offer. And he said he was OK with me presenting it as long as you guys are OK with this. You OK if I spend a few minutes going over it? And then he wait. And if they don't say anything, sometimes they won't, you say, cool, I already know all this stuff. So if you guys don't want it, I'm cool. I can just go home. I'm good. And then they're all like, no, no, no. They always do it. So almost every time they'll say yes when you ask permission and the times that they don't, that's how you do it. Like, OK, sure, because I already know this stuff. I'm good. I can go right now. And they always say something because they feel bad. But as soon as they say that, as soon as they say yes and give you permission to sell, nobody moves out of their seats. It's the craziest thing in the world. They will all sit there, and then they'll listen to you as you make your special offer. But if you don't ask them permission, it's this awkward, weird thing. So I'll do it one more time real quick. So how many of you guys feel a little bit like this? Cool. Because of that, we put together a really special offer to help you take all the stuff you figured out in the last 90 minutes and be able to go home and be able to implement this so you can have the same results in your business, in your life, in your health, whatever your business is. What I'm doing right here is I'm trying to create a constraint that later my bonus can let go of that constraint. Okay? So when I get to the end of my stack, remember this slide, because the end of the stack, what my bonus is is for any of you guys who get started in the next 48 hours, the first 50 or whatever my thing is, I'm going to take this constraint off. Okay? Because this is pretty cool. Cool. I can have 70 funnels, 20,000 contacts, 100,000 monthly visitors, and more. But in their mind, the thing is like, what if I create 100 funnels? I'm going to make this impossible. And they all freak out. None of them ever do, but they always are like, oh, I don't want any limits. So I try to introduce constraint here so I can take that constraint off later. Does that make sense? Okay? And that's like the key. So I'm going through here, and it's just like, this is where you're going to get this, this, this, and they're all excited. And in the back of the head, they're like, what happens if I make funnels number 71 or if I have 30,000 contacts? So what's going to happen? And so when I release that constraint at the end, that's when they go nuts. That's when the, that's when the masses start. That's when people are sprinting over the table, jumping over themselves. So I don't know if this is the best place to say this but I've been around Russell for a long time and he said something really key to you guys that if I can tell you one thing that you want to learn from Russell is that he said in our inner circle meeting it's the biggest all I've had in a long time but he says he creates chaos in his life to force himself to do things. He's not telling you to do anything that he honestly doesn't do but it's a really cool lesson that a lot of us don't do. We do not create chaos because we're scared shitless of it because as soon as you do you have to force yourself to force and encourage. their customers like I care about you guys I want to be here for the long haul right now we spend over two hundred fifty thousand dollars a month on programmers to make sure the software gets better every single day for you okay I've got over a hundred full-time support people here to support you because we care about you okay so I didn't want to go with this I don't want to keep as possible I could sell a lot more but it's not going to serve you okay so my second option was it obviously requires a little higher a little higher investment on your side but the cool thing is that in exchange that I can devote more resources to help guarantee your success and they're like cool thank you for charging me more okay I want them to be like yeah you're right they don't do they don't make this cheap please I don't want to go I don't want to go bankrupt on this whole thing to collapse right I'm getting them to agree that like man you should charge me because it's I want this to be awesome I also was able to slip in the words that guarantee your success which is awesome anyway I can't guarantee their success legally but in exchange I can devote more resources to help guarantee your success your subconscious mind just heard guarantee your success like oh I want to guarantee my success no I'm telling you guys yeah I say but exchange that I can help because this is this is the legal statement in exchange for that I can I can devote more resource sources to help guarantee your success like that's a legally compliant statement but your subconscious mind here is Guarantee success and it's like sweet.
